About Us

A small pre-school with a big heart

Based in Church Preen Village Hall, just opposite Church Preen Primary School. We've been part of this beautiful Shropshire community for over 20 years.

🌳

Rural Shropshire setting

The Ape Dale is our natural classroom — including a shared forest school space with Church Preen Primary School right next door.

👨‍👩‍👧

Parent committee run

We're a registered charity (No. 1033121), run by a dedicated parent committee — every family has a voice in how we work.

🎓

EYFS play-based learning

Following the Early Years Foundation Stage framework, with qualified staff who truly know every child as an individual.

🤝

Strong school links

Shared lunches and activities with Church Preen Primary ensures every transition to school is smooth and confident.

Come and visit us →

Our Team

Caring, qualified & passionate

Our small, dedicated team knows every child by name — and that makes all the difference.

Manager
Maria Reynolds
Pre-School Manager & Play Leader

Maria leads with warmth and vision, with extensive experience in EYFS, safeguarding, and curriculum planning. She is the first point of contact for new families.

Level 3 Qualified Paediatric First Aid Safeguarding Lead
Deputy
Jemma Pugh
Deputy Manager

Jemma brings enthusiasm, reliability, and a deeply caring approach to her role. She excels at supporting individual needs and planning engaging, play-based activities.

Level 3 Qualified Paediatric First Aid SEND Support
What We Do

Learning through play & adventure

Every session is a new discovery. Here's what makes Church Preen truly special.

🌲 Outdoors

Forest School

Children explore, build dens, identify wildlife and take safe risks in nature — using the shared space at Church Preen Primary. Rain or shine, every session builds resilience, curiosity and a deep love of the natural world.

📖 Literacy

Phonics & Early Literacy

Gentle, multisensory phonics through songs, rhymes, treasure hunts and puppets. Fun, pressure-free and always child-paced — giving every child a confident, joyful start with language.

🎨 Creative

Arts, Crafts & Making

Painting, sculpting, seasonal projects — children express themselves freely while building fine motor skills and creative confidence in our well-resourced indoor space.

👶 Community

Parent & Toddler Group

A warm, informal gathering for younger children and their grown-ups — a lovely way to meet local families and discover our setting before they're ready to join us.

Joining Us

Admissions

We accept applications year-round, with places on a first-come, first-served basis. We'd love you to come and visit first.

How to Apply

1

Get in touch

Email info@churchpreenpreschool.co.uk or call 01694 771797 to express interest and request a registration pack.

2

Visit us

Come for a relaxed, no-obligation look around. Meet the team and see what a typical day looks like for yourself.

3

Register

Complete and return your registration form. Places are allocated on a first-come, first-served basis.

💰 Free Funding Available

  • All 3 & 4-year-olds: 15 free hours per week
  • Working parents (eligible): 30 free hours per week
  • Some 2-year-olds may also qualify — please ask us
📋 Registration Form (PDF) 💷 Funding Eligibility Form (PDF) ♿ SEN Support Form (PDF) 🔒 Privacy Notice (PDF)

Common Questions

When can my child start?
Children can join from age 2, starting at the beginning of the next term after they become eligible.
What funding is available?
All 3 & 4-year-olds receive 15 free hours per week. Working parents who each earn at least National Minimum Wage for 16hrs/week (and under £100,000/year) may qualify for 30 hours. Some 2-year-olds also qualify.
What sessions are available?
We run Monday to Wednesday, 9am–3pm. Sessions can be morning, afternoon, or full-day. We recommend a minimum of 4 sessions per week to help children settle well.
Do you support SEN children?
Absolutely. We work closely with families and follow the SEN Code of Practice to ensure every child gets the right support.
How long will my child attend?
Until transition to Reception class in the September after their 4th birthday. We work hard to make this as smooth and positive as possible.
What is the parent and toddler group?
We run a friendly parent and toddler group alongside our sessions — a lovely way to meet local families and introduce younger children to our setting before they're ready to join.

Governance

Policies & Documents

Reviewed annually. Available to view at the pre-school or by request from Maria.

Required EYFS Policies

  • Safeguarding and Child Protection
  • Complaints Procedure
  • Health and Safety
  • Administering Medicines
  • Food and Drink
  • Managing Behaviour
  • Suitable People (Recruitment & Vetting)
  • Staff Qualifications, Training, Support & Skills
  • Key Person
  • Staff:Child Ratios
  • SEND
  • Information & Records
  • Premises, Environment & Equipment

Additional Policies

  • Admissions
  • Equality, Diversity & Inclusion
  • Parental Partnership
  • Observation, Assessment & Planning
  • Curriculum & Learning
  • Intimate Care (Nappy Changing/Toileting)
  • Attendance & Absence
  • Outings & Trips
  • Uncollected/Lost Child
  • Confidentiality
  • ICT, E-Safety & Social Media
  • Sun Protection & Outdoor Play
  • Fees & Funding
📩 To request any policy document, contact Maria Reynolds: maria@churchpreenpreschool.org.uk or call 01694 771797.
